mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strong household consumption, driving corporate earnings growth and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS vs forward 20-day returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or the hit rate of positive returns when RSAFS increases is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and prompts the Fed to tighten, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly rate of change exceeds 0.5%
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL monthly change >0.5%, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high payroll employment growth and low unemployment indicate a tight labor market that forces the Fed into restrictive policy, ultimately reducing equity prices over the long term.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS is above 160,000 (monthly change) and UNRATE is below 4%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals economic expansion and improving corporate profitability, boosting S&P 500 returns, but this positive effect is strongest in post-recession recovery regimes when capacity slack exists.
failure_condition: The positive correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 20-day returns is not statistically significant (rank IC <0.05) during the identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strength in the residential investment sector, a leading indicator of economic growth, which supports higher equity prices.
failure_condition: The predictive rank IC of HOUST on forward 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold (e.g., 4.5%), financial conditions become restrictive, raising the cost of capital and reducing equity demand, causing short-term negative returns.
failure_condition: Instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces industrial production growth, demand pressures build, leading to higher inflation expectations and tighter monetary policy, which is negative for equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth minus INDPRO growth is positive, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets, an increase in the unemployment rate signals the onset of contractionary conditions, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.1pp in a month, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances.
